reasoning: NFL owners tabled the initial April vote to ban the "tush push" (which was split 16-16, needing 24 votes) to the upcoming Spring League Meeting on May 20-21, 2025. The deadline for this question is June 1, so the meeting securely falls within the timeline. Following the April tie, the Green Bay Packers reportedly rewrote the proposal to enact a broader prohibition on pushing/pulling the runner (reverting to pre-2004 rules) specifically to attract the necessary holdout votes. NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell has publicly expressed support for this change and explicitly stated in late April that he expects the owners to reach a "consensus" at the May meetings. Further, recent reports from early May project the ban is on the verge of passing, with the league office aggressively lobbying for it. Punting an issue to the May meeting for rewording with explicit Commissioner backing typically results in passage. There remains a small chance the required 24 votes fall short, but all signs point to affirmative resolution.
